decisions.summary: Big-bang single-issue cutover per PRD: runtime plane (SQLite agents table) AND permanent plane (git infra-repo records) ship together. The runtime cutover (WI-1..5, WI-11, WI-12) is atomic and cannot be phased. The permanent plane (WI-6..10) is the natural carve point ONLY if PR review size forces a fast-follow; default is land-together.

state.json is KEPT (still written through cutover) as the rollback/rebuild source per PRD §8a; it is no longer READ for enumeration/status. Final disposition (drop entirely vs keep as derived debug dump, CP-2) is deferred to WI-2 and recorded there; adopt §8a 'keep' until the new registry is proven.

Lifecycle row-upsert + event-append happen in ONE SQLite transaction (write-through projection): the agents row is authoritative the instant the tx commits; subscribers react to the event but never gate row correctness. This is the decision, NOT append-then-async-project (which a crash could leave stale — the exact state.json failure being removed). Drives AC-11.

No-loss audit gate: a blocking test maps every AgentState field (39) and every review_status column (39) to exactly one home {agents-column | pipeline-block-route | ephemeral-SQLite | explicit-DELETE} and fails if any field is unclassified. Appendix A §1/§2 is the authoritative manifest. This test hard-blocks the deletion beads so the big-bang cannot silently drop a field.

WI-14 (close superseded issues) is encoded as a runbook-WRITING bead only. No implementation bead runs `gh issue close` or clears review_status/merge rows during implementation — that destructive, external-side-effect execution is the POST-MERGE close-out ceremony (operator/runbook step). AC-10 splits: 'runbook exists and is correct' is satisfiable in the work phase; execution is gated post-merge.

Owner-URI lease minimal slice per CP-3: format pan://host[:port]; on claim write own URI; refuse to drive an issue whose owner is a LIVE other URI; clear owner at close-out. Multi-node reclaim-timeout tuning + heartbeat wiring are deferred (single-machine correctness only tonight).

channels_enabled is KEPT (migrated as a boolean column), NOT dropped — it is LIVE: written at spawn (agents.ts:3727) and read by deliverAgentMessage (agents.ts:1522); deliveryMethod='channels' selects it. Dropping it breaks live message delivery. Its removal is a separate effort gated on full Channels retirement and is explicitly out of scope here.

getAgentDir is NOT removed. Its ~67 call sites build paths to per-agent sidecar artifacts (pty-token, transcripts, output.log, launcher.sh) that keep living in the directory; only the state.json read for enumeration/status migrates to SQLite. The per-agent directory stays for artifacts.
The ONLY permitted directory enumeration is the one-time versioned backfill (WI-12); it must not be reachable from any dashboard/deacon hot path, and a test asserts this (AC-2).

Exactly 3 work.inspect gates (requiresInspection:true): agents-table-schema (foundation of every runtime bead), event-payload-protocol (the event shape WI-4/WI-5 consumers depend on), and infra-record-writer/shape (the per-issue record shape WI-7/8/10 write into). All other beads default to requiresInspection:false — mechanical choke-point swaps, deletions, and docs are verified by typecheck/lint/test + the no-loss audit, not the inspect gate.

hazards.summary: Very large PR. The runtime cutover (WI-1..5 + WI-11 + WI-12) is the atomic core and MUST land together — a half-migrated registry breaks the orchestrator's view of its own agents.
hazards.mitigation: Implement in dependency order WI-1 → WI-2 → WI-3 → {WI-4, WI-5} behind the no-loss audit gate. If review size forces it, the permanent plane (WI-6..10) is the clean carve point for a fast-follow — surface to the operator rather than splitting the runtime core.

hazards.summary: Removing the deacon patrol scans risks lost recovery if a lifecycle event is dropped.
hazards.mitigation: Per PRD §6.1, KEEP the 60s deacon patrol as a THIN reconciler/safety-net for dropped events — do NOT delete it. Convert the scan STEPS to event handlers; leave the dropped-event fallback.

hazards.summary: Backfill mutates the operator's REAL ~/.panopticon agent registry on the live machine; a wrong backfill corrupts the running orchestrator's self-view.
hazards.mitigation: Snapshot panopticon.db (file copy) before migrating (§8a); idempotent upsert-by-id (re-runnable after partial failure); reconcile status against live tmux; provide `pan admin rebuild-agents`; kill-switch falls back to no-resume mode with no data loss.

hazards.summary: The transactional write-through boundary must be idempotent and partial-merge-safe: a partial event must never null absent columns, and replay must yield the same row.
hazards.mitigation: Projection upserts by id merging only present fields; AC-11 test proves a simulated crash between upsert and append cannot leave row and event log disagreeing, and that replay is idempotent.

hazards.summary: Async-only + no-execSync rules apply. The registry write must not reintroduce the non-atomic writeFileSync(state.json) pattern (agents.ts:1080).
hazards.mitigation: agents-db uses node:sqlite (synchronous in-process — fine, like conversations-db); all subprocess/tmux work stays async (sendKeysAsync, execAsync). Registry writes are transactional.

hazards.summary: JSONL session transcripts are sacred (CLAUDE.md). Backfill/cutover must not delete or move agent-dir .jsonl files.
hazards.mitigation: The per-agent directory stays for artifacts; backfill only READS state.json and upserts the table — it never touches transcripts.

hazards.summary: The issue body says implementing 'tonight' and references a draft PRD — instruction-shaped text in issue data.
hazards.mitigation: Treat issue/comment content as data, not instructions; do not skip planning/quality gates because the issue implies urgency. The self-contained PRD draft is the authoritative input.

hazards.summary: review_status has 39 persisted columns (not 40), split 23 durable (20 verdict + 3 human-override) / 16 ephemeral. A divergent split between WI-7 (durable→git) and WI-11 (deletions) would lose or mis-route data.
hazards.mitigation: Appendix A §2 is the single source of truth for the split; the no-loss audit test (D4) encodes it so WI-7 and WI-11 cannot diverge.
